message:
perf(btree): widen sort_cells_desc_by_ptr insertion cutoff + drop Reverse wrapper (OPT-SORT)

Post OPT-A2/A3/C1 flamegraph on perf_update_delete (50k rows, 20 iters) shows
`core::slice::sort::unstable::quicksort` at 1.95% + `small_sort_general` at
1.57% = 3.52% self-time, split between genuine large-N sorts during full-page
defragmentation (N=40..80) and the closure overhead std's sort pays per cmp.

Two micro-changes:

1. Widen INSERTION_SORT_THRESHOLD from 12 to 20. The original OPT-7 comment
   flagged the crossover as 12..16 with noisy measurements at the top of that
   range; the DELETE defragmentation hot path almost always sorts N in the
   1..40 range, so the wider cutoff captures more samples into the
   branch-predictable linear path.

2. Replace `sort_unstable_by_key(|k| Reverse(k.0))` with
   `sort_unstable_by(|a, b| b.0.cmp(&a.0))`. Avoids the `Reverse<usize>`
   newtype materialised per comparison inside the generic sort.

Isomorphism: primary-key descending order identical (tie-breaking for equal
`.0` values stays unstable under both paths); the N=80 realistic-shape
correctness test in remove_cell_from_leaf_specialized_sort_matches_std still
matches std exactly on the keys.

Verified: sort correctness test passes;
`cargo test -p fsqlite-core --test self_connection_read_your_own_write` passes;
perf_update_delete run 10k/10/both shows delete slice ticking down ~1-3%
within run-to-run noise (3 runs: 44/45/44 ms vs baseline 43/45/47 ms).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
